summary:
The appropriate statistical method here involves using an independent samples t-test, which will help determine if there’s a meaningful difference in average birth weight based on mothers' consumption patterns regarding iron supplementation during pregnancy.
To refine it slightly while keeping within your constraints:

The investigation requires comparing mean birthweights between infants whose mothers took iron supplements vs. did not; thus, an *unpaired/独立样本*t-检验适用于此情景，以评估两组间平均出生体重是否存在显著差异。 To ensure clarity and maintain brevity strictly at around two sentences as requested but with minor adjustments due to language limitations: 

An independent samples t-test suits best for assessing differences in mean birthweight between children born to mothers consuming iron supplements compared to non-consumers. This approach effectively evaluates potential associations given the nature of both variables involved—continuous birthweight measurements against dichotomous supplement usage status.
